title: S5: migrate worktree-parallelism — the one genuinely shapeless area, via a new narrative-sections scheme
action: This is the ONLY area of the eleven with no anchor-bearing sections: docs/specs/worktree-parallelism.md (225 lines) has 10 narrative '## ' headings (What problem this solves / The trust model / Registering a worktree / Entering / Returning / Routing rule / Cross-worktree holds / The three tiers / Boundary / Where it lives) and zero numbered B*/R*/E*/P* anchors. F9 forbids forcing it into ba-nine-section. INSTEAD, add a THIRD scheme to okf_migrate.mjs: `narrative-sections` — the anchors ARE the source's own '## ' headings, derived mechanically from the heading text (slugified, e.g. S-the-trust-model), never invented. This mirrors the existing flat-pattern-list precedent, which already treats '## [YYYYMMDD] title' headings as anchors. The anchor's TEXT for fidelity purposes is that section's body up to the next heading. Then run the normal loop: STEP 0 capture the pin (scheme narrative-sections, expected_counts with the heading count and unparsed_blocks) with the verbatim source copy hash-verified; STEP 1 author concepts under docs/knowledge/areas/worktree-parallelism/ splitting by topic, every heading-anchor owned by exactly one concept, terminology verbatim, canonical frontmatter; STEP 2 pointer stub with the full anchor map (D37) mapping each heading to its concept, reading-map row rewired; STEP 3 add --check worktree-parallelism to EXTRA_SUITES + MANDATORY_SUITE_ARGS; STEP 4 index regen, log.md entry, full chain (74 suites). RED-FIRST: add the scheme's tests (a heading becomes an anchor; a '###' subheading does NOT; a source with zero '## ' headings is refused rather than passed 0/0) and capture them red before implementing; then the usual one-anchor-unassigned gate red. IMPORTANT: the three shipped schemes must stay strict no-ops — re-verify all 9 existing pins after adding the scheme.

truths: red evidence: the narrative-sections scheme tests failed before implementation (verbatim), plus the one-anchor-unassigned gate red
STRICT NO-OP: all 9 pre-existing pins still derive their exact expected_counts after the scheme is added — --verify-pins output quoted
coverage: every '## ' heading of the pinned blob owned by exactly one concept, 0 duplicated, 0 lost
fidelity min/median quoted above the 0.60 floor with no concept edited
a source with zero '## ' headings is REFUSED under this scheme, never passed 0/0
knowledge check 0/0; index --check 0 stale; blob_sha verified
prohibitions: never invent numbered ids for a source that has none (D10) — the headings are the anchors
never force this area into ba-nine-section (F9)
never let the new scheme change any existing pin's derived counts
never edit a concept or lower a threshold to clear the floor
